user.title: Power Insight
user.description:
Power Insight tracks the real-time power flow, electricity costs, savings, and CO₂ impact of your home's energy mix — grid, solar, batteries, and individual consumers in one place.

Give this configuration a name to get started. After setup, use the **Add device** button to connect your grid meter, PV inverter, battery system, and any consumers you want to track. Then open the integration's options to choose which sensors to create.

data_description.name: A short name for this energy-mix configuration, for example "Home" or "Office". Used as the device name in Home Assistant.

preset.title: Choose your sensor set
preset.description:
Pick a starting point for which sensors Power Insight creates. Each preset builds on the previous one — you can always change this later in the integration's **Options**.

Not sure which to pick? **Recommended** is a great starting point for most homes: it covers the sensors people use most without adding complexity you may not need yet.

data_description.preset:
**Minimal** — Distribution ratios (%), power-source attribution, and financial-return sensors. A lightweight overview of your energy mix.

**Recommended** — Adds distribution power (W), charging-source attribution, and running totals for export compensation, costs and savings. The sweet spot for most installations.

**Extended** — Also adds real-time cost, savings and export-compensation rate sensors (€/h or $/h) plus levelized cost sensors. Cost sensors need a live price entity on your grid adapter; levelized sensors need lifetime production and cost values per device.

You can fine-tune individual sensors at any time in the integration's Options.

error.invalid_name: The name cannot be empty or invalid.

init.title: Power Insight options
init.description:
Choose a preset to apply the same sensor selection to all your devices, or choose **Custom** to configure each device type individually on the pages that follow.

Presets apply instantly — no extra pages. Choosing **Custom** walks you through one page per device class (combined, grid, PV, battery, consumers) so you can mix and match.

Any sensors you turn off here are disabled in Home Assistant but not deleted, so historical data is preserved. You can re-enable them at any time.

data_description.preset:
**Minimal** — Distribution ratios and financial-return sensors only.
**Recommended** — Adds distribution power, source attribution, and running totals for costs, savings and export compensation.
**Extended** — Also adds real-time cost/savings rate sensors and levelized cost sensors (levelized needs lifetime values per device).
**Custom** — Configure each device type individually on the following pages.
data_description.debug_power_entities: Expose the raw internal power values used for calculations as additional sensors. Useful for diagnosing unexpected readings. Leave off unless you are troubleshooting.

combined.title: Combined sensors
combined.description:
These sensors aggregate power and costs **across your whole home** — they sum up all your devices and give you a single number for each metric.

For example, the combined self-consumption power sensor shows how many Watts of locally-produced power (solar + battery discharge) your home is currently using instead of drawing from the grid.

data_description.distribution_power: Creates one sensor per power flow type (self-consumption, grid import, grid export, etc.) showing the current power in Watts. These are the fundamental building blocks — other sensors are derived from them.
data_description.distribution_ratios: Creates percentage sensors that show each power flow as a share of total home power. For example: "currently 65 % of my home load comes from solar." Useful for dashboards.

data_description.cost_method:
**None** — no cost sensors created.
**Standard** — calculates electricity cost using the live grid import price per kWh from your grid adapter. Requires a price entity on the grid adapter.
**Levelized** — calculates cost using each device's lifetime cost per kWh (LCOE/LCOS). Requires lifetime production and cost values set per device.
**Both** — creates a pair of sensors for each method.
data_description.accumulate_costs: Creates additional sensors that add up the cost over time (running total in your currency). The total is stored in Home Assistant's recorder and survives restarts.

data_description.savings_method:
Cost savings measure how much money you avoid spending on grid electricity by self-consuming locally-produced power.

**None** — no savings sensors.
**Standard** — savings calculated using the live grid price. Requires a price entity on the grid adapter.
**Levelized** — savings calculated using each device's LCOE as the baseline. Requires lifetime values per device.
**Both** — creates sensors for both methods.
data_description.accumulate_savings: Creates sensors that accumulate total savings over time. The total is stored in Home Assistant's recorder and survives restarts.

grid.title: Grid sensors
grid.description:
These sensors describe your **grid connection** — how much power you are importing from or exporting to the grid, what it costs, and what you earn from exports.

Grid sensors only appear in Custom mode when a grid adapter is configured.

data_description.distribution_power: Creates separate import-power and export-power sensors in Watts. Import is power you buy from the grid; export is surplus power you send back.
data_description.distribution_ratios: Creates sensors showing grid import and export as a percentage of your total home power flow. For example: "currently 40 % of my home load is coming from the grid."
data_description.distribution_shares: Creates sensors showing what fraction of all grid throughput is import vs. export. For example: "70 % of the power that flowed through my grid connection today was imports."

data_description.export_compensation_rate: A real-time sensor (in your currency per hour) showing how fast you are currently earning money from power exported to the grid. Calculated from the current export power × your export compensation rate.
data_description.export_compensation_total: A running total of all export compensation earned since the sensor was created. Stored in the recorder and survives restarts.

data_description.cost_method:
**None** — no cost sensors for the grid connection.
**Standard** — calculates the cost of grid imports using the live price per kWh from your grid adapter's price entity. Requires a price entity configured on the grid adapter.

(Grid electricity has no levelized cost — that concept applies only to devices you own, like solar panels and batteries.)
data_description.accumulate_costs: Creates a sensor that accumulates the total import cost over time (running total in your currency). Stored in the recorder and survives restarts.

pv_system.title: PV system sensors
pv_system.description:
These sensors describe your **solar PV systems** — how much power they produce, how much of that production you self-consume vs. export, and the financial impact.

If you have multiple PV systems, these settings apply to all of them. Each system gets its own set of sensors.

data_description.distribution_power: Creates sensors in Watts for PV production (total output), self-consumption (used directly in your home), and export (surplus sent to the grid).
data_description.distribution_ratios: Creates sensors showing PV production as a percentage of total home power flow. For example: "solar is currently covering 80 % of my home load."
data_description.distribution_shares: Creates sensors showing what fraction of total PV production is self-consumed vs. exported. For example: "60 % of my solar output is going directly into the home, 40 % is exported."

data_description.export_compensation_rate: A real-time sensor showing how fast you are currently earning money from PV power exported to the grid (your currency per hour).
data_description.export_compensation_total: Running total of all export compensation earned from this PV system. Stored in the recorder and survives restarts.

data_description.cost_method:
**None** — no cost sensors.
**Standard** — cost calculated using the live grid price (treating self-consumed solar as though it were bought at the grid rate).
**Levelized** — cost calculated using the system's lifetime cost per kWh (LCOE), derived from the total cost and expected lifetime production you set per device. Requires lifetime values.
**Both** — creates sensors for both methods.
data_description.accumulate_costs: Creates sensors that accumulate total cost over time. Stored in the recorder and survives restarts.

data_description.savings_method:
Savings represent the money you avoid spending on grid electricity by self-consuming PV power.

**None** — no savings sensors.
**Standard** — savings calculated using the live grid price. Requires a price entity on the grid adapter.
**Levelized** — savings calculated using the PV system's LCOE as the baseline cost. Requires lifetime values.
**Both** — creates sensors for both methods.
data_description.accumulate_savings: Creates sensors that accumulate total savings over time. Stored in the recorder and survives restarts.

battery.title: Battery sensors
battery.description:
These sensors describe your **battery storage systems** — charge and discharge power, which sources charged the battery (grid vs. solar), and the levelized cost of storage.

If you have multiple batteries, these settings apply to all of them.

data_description.distribution_power: Creates separate charge-power and discharge-power sensors in Watts.
data_description.distribution_ratios: Creates sensors showing battery charge/discharge as a percentage of total home power flow.
data_description.distribution_shares: Creates sensors showing what fraction of total battery throughput is charging vs. discharging.
data_description.charging_source_shares: Creates sensors showing how much of the battery's current charging energy comes from each configured source (grid, PV system). For example: "currently 70 % of the charging power is coming from solar, 30 % from the grid." Only sources you selected in the battery adapter's configuration appear here.

data_description.export_compensation_rate: A real-time sensor showing how fast you are currently earning money from battery power exported to the grid.
data_description.export_compensation_total: Running total of all export compensation earned from this battery. Stored in the recorder and survives restarts.

data_description.cost_method:
**None** — no cost sensors.
**Standard** — cost calculated using the live grid price.
**Levelized** — cost calculated using the battery's levelized cost of storage (LCOS), derived from the total lifetime cost and expected lifetime throughput you set per device. Requires lifetime values.
**Both** — creates sensors for both methods.
data_description.accumulate_costs: Creates sensors that accumulate total cost over time. Stored in the recorder and survives restarts.

data_description.savings_method:
Savings represent the money you avoid spending on grid electricity by discharging the battery instead.

**None** — no savings sensors.
**Standard** — savings calculated using the live grid price.
**Levelized** — savings calculated using the battery's LCOS as the baseline. Requires lifetime values.
**Both** — creates sensors for both methods.
data_description.accumulate_savings: Creates sensors that accumulate total savings over time. Stored in the recorder and survives restarts.

consumer.title: Consumer sensors
consumer.description:
These sensors describe your **electrical consumers** (appliances, EV chargers, heat pumps, etc.) — how much power they draw and where that power comes from.

If you have multiple consumers, these settings apply to all of them.

data_description.power_source_shares: Creates sensors showing what fraction of this consumer's power currently comes from each source in your home (grid, solar, battery). For example: "the heat pump is currently running 55 % on solar power." Power Insight infers the source mix from the real-time state of all your adapters.

data_description.cost_method:
**None** — no cost sensors for this consumer.
**Standard** — cost calculated using the live grid price, weighted by the source mix. Requires a price entity on the grid adapter.
**Levelized** — cost calculated using the levelized cost per kWh of each source in the mix. Requires lifetime values on each generating device.
**Both** — creates sensors for both methods.

error.reconfigure_adapters_first: These devices are missing data required by your selection: {adapters_needing_reconfigure}. Open each device's **Reconfigure** page to supply the missing values (for example, an electricity price entity for cost sensors, or lifetime production and cost for levelized sensors), then save the options again.

user.title: Add Device
user.description: Choose the type of device you want to add to your energy mix.

configure.title: Configure {adapter_type}
configure.description: Set up your {adapter_type}. Which fields are required depends on the sensors you enabled in the integration's options — for example, cost sensors need a price entity and levelized sensors need lifetime values.

data_description.name: A unique name for this device, for example "Rooftop Solar" or "Home Battery". Used as the device name in Home Assistant.
data_description.power_entity: Sensor that reports this device's power in W, kW or MW. Sign convention: Grid — positive = import, negative = export. PV and battery — positive = producing/discharging, negative = consuming/charging. Use **Invert power direction** if your sensor uses the opposite sign.
data_description.power_entity_inverted: Turn on if your sensor reports power with the opposite sign to the convention described above.
data_description.grid_electricity_price_entity: Sensor or input number with the current grid import price per kWh, in your Home Assistant currency. Required when any cost or savings sensors are enabled.
data_description.co2_intensity_entity: Sensor reporting the current grid CO₂ intensity in g/kWh (for example from the Electricity Maps integration). Required when CO₂ sensors are enabled.
data_description.exports_power: Turn on if this device can feed surplus power back to the grid. Enables export-compensation tracking when the corresponding option is active.
data_description.export_compensation: The amount you are paid per kWh of power exported to the grid, in your currency. Required when export-compensation or savings sensors are enabled.
data_description.battery_efficiency: Round-trip efficiency in percent — the share of energy stored that you actually get back when discharging (typical values: 85–95 %). Used to correctly attribute charging losses to their source.
data_description.charge_from_adapters: Select which sources this battery can charge from — the grid and/or specific PV systems you have configured. Power Insight uses this to trace how much of the battery's stored energy came from each source. At least one source is required; a battery with none cannot charge, so to draw from your whole mix select every source.
data_description.power_from_adapters: Optionally restrict which sources power this consumer — the grid and/or specific PV systems. Useful for a smart plug set to run only on excess solar. Leave empty to draw from your home's general power mix.
data_description.lifetime_production: Total energy in kWh you expect this device to produce (or store) over its entire lifetime. Combined with the total lifetime cost to derive the levelized (per-kWh) cost. Required when any levelized sensor is enabled.
data_description.lifetime_cost: Total purchase, installation, and expected maintenance cost of this device over its lifetime, in your currency. Combined with lifetime production to derive the levelized cost per kWh. Required when any levelized cost sensor is enabled.
data_description.co2_footprint: Total CO₂ emitted to manufacture and install this device (in kg). Used to derive a levelized CO₂ intensity per kWh produced. Required when any levelized CO₂ sensor is enabled.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure {adapter_type}
reconfigure.description: Update this device's configuration. If you change the lifetime cost or lifetime production values, Power Insight applies a correction factor that retroactively rescales this device's already-recorded levelized values to stay consistent.

data_description.power_entity: Sensor that reports this device's power in W, kW or MW.
data_description.power_entity_inverted: Turn on if your sensor reports power with the opposite sign to the expected convention.
data_description.grid_electricity_price_entity: Sensor or input number with the current grid import price per kWh. Required when cost or savings sensors are enabled.
data_description.co2_intensity_entity: Sensor with the current grid CO₂ intensity in g/kWh. Required when CO₂ sensors are enabled.
data_description.charge_from_adapters: Select which sources (the grid and/or your PV systems) this battery charges from. At least one is required — a battery with no charge source cannot charge; select every source to draw from your whole mix.
data_description.power_from_adapters: Select which sources (the grid and/or your PV systems) power this consumer. Leave empty to draw from the general mix.
data_description.lifetime_production: Total energy (kWh) you expect this device to produce over its whole lifetime. Changing this value rescales the device's already-recorded levelized values.
data_description.lifetime_cost: Total purchase and installation cost of this device over its lifetime, in your currency. Changing this value rescales the device's already-recorded levelized values.

error.invalid_name: The name cannot be empty or invalid.
error.name_not_unique: A device with this name already exists. Please choose a unique name.
error.invalid_power_entity: The selected entity does not exist or does not report power (W, kW, MW).
error.invalid_price_entity: The selected entity does not exist or is unavailable.
error.invalid_co2_entity: The selected entity does not exist or is unavailable.
error.required: This field is required with the sensors you currently have enabled.
error.charge_sources_required: Select at least one source this battery can charge from. A battery with no charge source cannot charge — to draw from your whole mix, select every source.

abort.grid_already_configured: A grid connection is already configured for this integration. Only one grid connection is supported per configuration.

no_grid_configured.title: Add a grid connection to {entry_title}
no_grid_configured.description:
**{entry_title}** has no grid connection yet, so Power Insight cannot calculate any costs, savings or power distribution — every device in your energy mix is measured against the grid.

To fix this:
1. Open **Settings → Devices & services** and select the Power Insight integration.
2. Click **Add device** and choose **Grid connection**.
3. Select the sensor that reports your home's grid power (positive = import, negative = export).

This warning clears automatically once a grid connection is added.

reconfigure_battery_adapters.title: Battery {battery_name} needs reconfiguration
reconfigure_battery_adapters.description: A charging source was added or removed. Please reconfigure **{battery_name}** so the correct charging sources are applied. Open the integration, find the device, and use the **Reconfigure** option.

battery_no_charge_source.title: Battery {battery_name} has no charge source
battery_no_charge_source.description:
**{battery_name}** has no charge source configured, so Power Insight cannot attribute its charging to any source. Open the integration, find the device, and use the **Reconfigure** option to select the sources (the grid and/or your PV systems) it charges from. To draw from your whole mix, select every source.

This warning clears automatically once a charge source is set.

set_value.name: Set value
set_value.description: Set the accumulated total of a Power Insight total/accumulation sensor, for example to carry over historical totals when adopting the integration.

value.name: Value
value.description: The new accumulated total to set.
